what_it_does: The Supabase CLI configuration file for the claude-design local development project. Declares project_id and configures every Supabase subsystem: API (ports/exposed schemas/max_rows), DB (Postgres 17, ports, pooler, migrations enabled, seed file), realtime, Studio, inbucket email testing, storage (50MiB limit, S3 protocol on, vector buckets on), a large auth block (site_url, JWT expiry, signup/anon rules, rate limits, email/SMS, MFA, external OAuth providers all disabled, third-party providers, OAuth server), edge_runtime (Deno 2, per_worker hot reload), analytics (off), and experimental (S3/OrioleDB, pgdelta diff engine enabled).
observations: project_id = 'gctunhsuwpaxeatwlmuv' ties this local config to a specific remote Supabase project
Non-default ports throughout (api 15421, db 15432, shadow 15433, studio 15423, inbucket 15424) — shifted off the standard 543xx range, likely to avoid a port clash
api.tls.enabled = false with commented-out cert/key paths pointing at ../certs/
All external OAuth (apple) and third-party providers (firebase/auth0/aws_cognito/clerk) and web3/solana are enabled = false
experimental.pgdelta.enabled = true (newer schema-diff engine over legacy migra)
storage.vector.enabled = true with max_buckets 10 / max_indexes 5; storage.s3_protocol enabled = true
Secrets consistently referenced via env() substitution (OPENAI_API_KEY, SENDGRID, twilio token, S3 keys) with DO-NOT-commit comments
standouts: Hardcoded project_id binding this dev config to a named remote project
Deliberate non-standard port range across all services
conventions: Follows the standard Supabase-generated config.toml template (extensive reference comments kept inline)
Follows the secret-via-env() convention rather than inline secrets
Most blocks left at defaults; only ports, db major_version, and a few toggles are customised
notes: Standard Supabase CLI config for the claude-design dev stack, pinned to remote project gctunhsuwpaxeatwlmuv and shifted onto a non-default 154xx port range.
questions: Why the non-default 154xx port range — is another Supabase stack running on the same host on 543xx?
Is the bound remote project gctunhsuwpaxeatwlmuv the production design connector home referenced in the migrations?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (no separately stated purpose; it is itself the config)
apparent_themes: local-dev-first then push to a named remote project
fail-closed defaults (anon sign-in off, external providers off)
secrets kept out of git via env substitution
intention_signals: Configures a self-contained local Supabase stack for the claude-design connector work, pinned to a specific remote project and arranged so it can coexist with another local stack via shifted ports.
novelty: The non-default port range and pgdelta-enabled diff engine are the distinct local choices; otherwise template-standard.
contribution: Brings the concrete environment binding for the claude-design Supabase stack — the project_id, the port map, and the subsystem toggles (vector storage, S3, realtime, auth posture) the rest of the migrations assume.
summary_for_embedding: Supabase CLI config.toml for the claude-design local dev stack, pinned to remote project gctunhsuwpaxeatwlmuv with non-default 154xx ports and customised db/auth/storage/experimental subsystem settings.
intention_for_embedding: To define and pin the local-then-remote Supabase environment for the claude-design connector project.
